message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to Option: touches Option.__init__ (param list + validation: must be non-negative int, only valid with multiple=True), Option.to_info_dict (add batch_size key), and Option.type_cast_value/processing logic to chunk multiple values into batches. Parameter.__init__ and Parameter.to_info_dict in same file are NOT touched by me. What constructs do you need in core.py/types.py?

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Options: (1) Option.__init__ - add validation for dict-based default (TypeError for non-str keys / non-dict values), (2) Option.get_help_extra - detect dict default and show '(dynamic)' in help, (3) new second-pass resolution step in Command.main() (inside the 'with self.make_context(...) as ctx:' block, before invoke) that resolves dict-based defaults using ctx.params/get_parameter_source for other options. Possibly touches types.py only for a type alias, TBD.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
code: Proposing merge approach: My batch_size feature adds:
1. New kwarg in signature: `batch_size: int | None = None,` (placed right after `multiple: bool = False,`)
2. Assignment `self.batch_size = batch_size` (placed right after `self.count = count` / count block, before `self.allow_from_autoenv = ...`)
3. Validation added inside the existing `if __debug__:` block at the end (after the `if self.count:` block):
    if self.batch_size is not None:
        if self.batch_size < 0:
            raise ValueError("'batch_size' must be non-negative.")
        if not self.multiple:
            raise TypeError("'batch_size' is only valid with 'multiple'.")

Can you share the EXACT lines you're inserting into Option.__init__ (signature addition if any, body assignment, and __debug__ validation) for the dict-based default check? I'll fold them into the same merged method so we agree on one final __init__ body. Also confirming: I will NOT touch Option.get_help_extra or Command.main, and you won't touch Option.to_info_dict (unless you need to) or Option.type_cast_value -- please confirm no overlap there.
note: requesting exact snippet to merge Option.__init__

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py
note: My feature touches: Option.__init__ (validate dict-based 'default': raise TypeError('Dynamic default keys must be strings') / TypeError('Dynamic default values must be dictionaries')), Option.get_default (return None early if isinstance(self.default, dict) so type casting doesn't choke on the dict during first pass), Option.get_help_extra (show '(dynamic)' when self.default is a dict, even though get_default returns None), and a new resolution step called from Command.main() after make_context() but before invoke(). Overlap: we both touch Option.__init__ (you add batch_size param+validation, I add dynamic-default validation). To keep our patches identical there, can you paste the EXACT current diff/snippet you plan for Option.__init__ (new param in signature, attribute assignment, and __debug__ validation lines)? I'll fold it together with mine into one merged __init__ body and send it back as a DRAFT for us to converge on.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/core.py
region: Option.__init__
code: EXACT signature change (add after the `multiple: bool = False,` line):
        batch_size: int | None = None,

EXACT body assignment (add immediately after `self.count = count` / count-block, i.e. right after:
        self.count = count
        if count:
            if type is None:
                self.type = types.IntRange(min=0)
            if default_is_missing:
                self.default = 0
insert:
        self.batch_size = batch_size

EXACT __debug__ validation (add at the end of the existing `if __debug__:` block, after the existing `if self.count:` sub-block):
            if self.batch_size is not None:
                if self.batch_size < 0:
                    raise ValueError("'batch_size' must be non-negative.")
                if not self.multiple:
                    raise TypeError("'batch_size' is only valid with 'multiple'.")
note: exact snippet for merge, confirming no overlap on get_default/get_help_extra/Command.main/to_info_dict/type_cast_value

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Implemented batch_size feature: Option.__init__ merged signature/attr/debug validation (identical to our agreed code, including your dict-default block), to_info_dict includes batch_size, new Option.type_cast_value override chunks multiple values into tuples of up to batch_size. No changes to get_default/get_help_extra/Command.main/type_cast_value's flat-tuple path beyond my override calling super(). Done on my end.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Implemented Dynamic Default Resolution: merged Option.__init__ (identical to agreed code, including your batch_size block), Option.get_default (returns None early for dict defaults so type casting doesn't choke during first pass), Option.get_help_extra (shows '(dynamic)' for dict defaults regardless of resolved value), and new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() called from main() between make_context() and invoke(). All existing tests pass. No changes to to_info_dict or type_cast_value. Done on my end.
